methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $60M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $1.0B - $11.0B.

private.name: SambaNova Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $11B

data.narrative:
Lace Lithography is a Series A-stage semiconductor company that has raised $60M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 11 private companies and 3 public peers (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) in the Chips & Semiconductors sector, including relevant private benchmarks such as SambaNova Systems, Etched, and Positron AI.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $60M total raised as a baseline proxy and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement, implying a $120M valuation. Peer-median analysis identifies three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables with a median valuation of $5.0B (range: $1.0B to $11.0B), reflecting the wide dispersion characteristic of early-stage semiconductor IP companies.

The triangulated analysis yields a low bound of $102M, a median of $2.6B, and a high bound of $5.8B. The three-fold gap between stage-progression and peer-median methodologies reflects material uncertainty around whether Lace's technology trajectory and market position align with high-outlier benchmarks like SambaNova ($11.0B) or lower-positioned peers ($1.0B). Key sensitivities include technical differentiation in lithography capability, path to commercialization timeline, and whether the addressable market supports multibillion-dollar outcomes. A post-Series A valuation in the $400M–$1.5B range would represent a calibrated midpoint absent additional technical or market validation.
